title: triticum turgidum subsp. volgense
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. volgense, also known as Volga wheat, is a subspecies of wheat native to the Volga-Ural region in Russia. It is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits associated with Volga wheat include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and even help manage diabetes. Traditionally, Volga wheat is prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used as a component in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures and decoctions.
